ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Sep-09-2025 Continued To Nov-18-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-07-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Freedman appeared by zoom for and with Danilo Quiazon (petitioner) by zoom.
Seth Skootsky appeared by zoom for Rico Quiazon (beneficiary).
Barbara Simon (proposed administrator) appeared by zoom in propria persona.

The court stated that it accepts the amended petition as an amendment to the initial petition as defined in CRC 7.3(4) and that it accepts the publication that is on file as it adequately provides notice. Mrs. Simon stated that she is going to be represented by Deborah Fox. The court finds that the 6124 presumption of revocation does not apply in light of the allegation that the original will was last in the possession of the Assessor's Office or the petitioner, but not the decedent. The Court granted the Amended Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed. The lost will dated November 22, 2011, is admitted to probate. Barbara Simon is appointed as the Administrator of the estate with will annexed with full authority. Bond is set at $337,000.00. Order for the same signed in open Court. A status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for September 9, 2025, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If either petition is filed two weeks prior, the status hearing will go off calendar. Amy Harrington is appointed probate referee.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Belen. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jun-26-2024 Continued To Aug-07-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar May-01-2024 Continued To Jun-26-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Freedman appeared by zoom for and with Danilo Quiazon (petitioner) by zoom.
Seth Skootsky appeared by zoom for Rico Quiazon (beneficiary).

Mr. Skootsky stated that he is here for the purpose of filing an objection, that Rico wants to serve as a co-executor, that in article 2 of the will the decedent consented to both of the sons serving as co-executors, and that there are other articles that only refer to Danilo which creates conflict that needs to be addressed. Mr. Skootsky also stated that the two brothers are in litigation in Vancouver British Columbia, that there is hostility between the two brothers and spoke of Danilo having mishandled the father's estate to Rico's detriment. In addition, Mr. Skootsky stated that there is a partition action here regarding the same property of the estate and that mediation in that matter is scheduled for June 14th. The court raised the question of how the brothers are going to be able to serve as co-executors with so much hostility between them. The Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to June 26, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Skootsky is to file his objections by May 15, 2024. If the objections are not filed by then, they will be waived.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-17-2024 Continued To May-01-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
